title: Shorea ovalis
intro: Shorea ovalis, also known as the Tengkawang tree, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia and commonly found in the rainforests of Malaysia and Indonesia. The bark and leaves of Shorea ovalis have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Shorea ovalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Shorea ovalis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, and is also used as a food ingredient in some Southeast Asian cuisines.
taxonomy: Shorea ovalis is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is a type of dipterocarp tree, which is native to Southeast Asia. Shorea ovalis is classified as a species of flowering plant, specifically within the order Malvales. The species is often found in tropical rainforests and is valued for its timber. Shorea ovalis is also known to be an important component of the region's biodiversity.
uses: Shorea ovalis, a species of tree native to Southeast Asia, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its bark is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In addition, the tree's leaves are used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Shorea ovalis.
